## Type `StateOfChargeLimit`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `upperSocLimit` | `Int` | Maximum level of charge allowed by the battery relative to its capacity (in percent). |
| `timestamp` | `String` | Time of the latest SoC limit reading. |
| `isLimitViolated` | `Boolean` | Whether or not target SoC exceeds upper SoC limit. |

## Type `StatementBillingDocumentConnectionTypeConnection`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

A statement is a billing document that contains all entries on a ledger during a period of time. A customer can understand how their ledger's balance has changed by looking at each statement in series.

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `pageInfo` | `PageInfo!` | Pagination data for this connection. |
| `edges` | `[StatementBillingDocumentConnectionTypeEdge]!` | Contains the nodes in this connection. |
| `totalCount` | `Int!` | Total number of nodes. |
| `edgeCount` | `Int!` | Number of nodes in the edge. |

## Type `StatementBillingDocumentConnectionTypeEdge`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

A Relay edge containing a `StatementBillingDocumentConnectionType` and its cursor.

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `node` | `StatementBillingDocumentType` | The item at the end of the edge |
| `cursor` | `String!` | A cursor for use in pagination |

## Type `StatementBillingDocumentType`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

A statement is a billing document that contains all entries on a ledger during a period of time. A customer can understand how their ledger's balance has changed by looking at each statement in series.

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `id` | `Int` | ID for the statement billing document. |
| `identifier` | `String` | The unique reference of the statement that can be used for identifying the statement externally. |
| `startAt` | `DateTime!` | The start of the statement's period. |
| `endAt` | `DateTime!` | The end of the statement's period. |
| `firstIssuedAt` | `DateTime` | The date and time the statement was sent to the customer. |
| `earliestChargeAt` | `DateTime` | The earliest charge date of the statement. |
| `latestChargeAt` | `DateTime` | The latest charge date of the statement. |
| `paymentDueDate` | `Date` | The date due for payment for the statement. |
| `isFinal` | `Boolean` | Whether this statement is marked as final. |
| `pdfUrl` | `String` | URL to the PDF of the statement. |
| `annulledBy` | `AnnulmentBillingDocumentType` | Billing document that annuls this statement. |
| `totalCharges` | `StatementTotalType` | The total amounts for all charges on the statement. |
| `totalCredits` | `StatementTotalType` | The total amounts for all credits on the statement. |
| `transactions(orderBy: TransactionsOrderBy, before: String, after: String, first: Int, last: Int)` | `BillTransactionConnectionTypeConnection` | Transactions on the statement |
| `documentDebtPosition` | `BillingDocumentPositionType` | Position of the billing document in the delinquent debt tracking system. |

### Field arguments

#### `transactions`

| Argument | Type | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `orderBy` | `TransactionsOrderBy` | The order in which to return the transactions. Default: `POSTED_DATE_DESC`. |
| `before` | `String` |  |
| `after` | `String` |  |
| `first` | `Int` |  |
| `last` | `Int` |  |

## Type `StatementTotalType`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `netTotal` | `Int` | The net total amount for the statement (in minor currency units). |
| `taxTotal` | `Int` | The total amount of tax on the statement (in minor currency units). |
| `grossTotal` | `Int` | The gross total amount for the statement (in minor currency units). |

## Type `StatementType`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

**Implements:** `BillInterface`, `ConstituentInterface`

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `id` | `ID` | The ID of the constituent bill. |
| `billType` | `BillTypeEnum` | The type of the bill. |
| `fromDate` | `Date` | The date of the constituent bill covered from. |
| `toDate` | `Date` | The date of the constituent bill covered to. |
| `temporaryUrl` | `String` | Requesting this field generates a temporary URL at which bill is available. This URL will expire after approximately an hour. It is intended for redirection purposes, NOT persistence in any form (e.g. inclusion in emails or the body of a web page). This field can raise an error with errorClass NOT_FOUND if the bill document has not been created/issued yet. This field is deprecated use 'attachments' field instead. Deprecated: The 'temporaryUrl' field is deprecated. This field is deprecated. Use the 'attachments' field instead. - Marked as deprecated on 2024-09-16. - Scheduled for removal on or after 2025-09-01. |
| `issuedDate` | `Date` | The date the bill was sent to the customer. |
| `attachments(before: String, after: String, first: Int, last: Int)` | `BillingAttachmentConnectionTypeConnection` |  |
| `reversalsAfterClose` | `StatementReversalsAfterClose!` | How many charges have been reversed after the close date. |
| `closingBalance` | `Int` | This field returns the closing balance of an issued statement. |
| `openingBalance` | `Int` | This field returns the opening balance of a statement. |
| `isExternalBill` | `Boolean` | Whether the bill originated in Kraken or externally. |
| `transactions(before: String, after: String, first: Int, last: Int)` | `TransactionConnectionTypeConnection` | Transactions on the bill. |
| `userId` | `Int` | Email recipient user ID. |
| `toAddress` | `String` | Email recipient address. |
| `paymentDueDate` | `Date` | The date the bill is due to be paid. |
| `consumptionStartDate` | `Date` | The first day of consumption that this statement includes. |
| `consumptionEndDate` | `Date` | The last day of consumption that this statement includes. |
| `isFinal` | `Boolean` | Whether this statement is marked as final. |
| `status` | `AccountStatementStatus` | Current status of the associated statement. |
| `heldStatus` | `HeldStatus` | Retrieve the held status of a account statement. |
| `totalCharges` | `StatementTotalType` | The total amounts for all charges on the statement. |
| `totalCredits` | `StatementTotalType` | The total amounts for all credits on the statement. |

### Field arguments

#### `attachments`

| Argument | Type | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `before` | `String` |  |
| `after` | `String` |  |
| `first` | `Int` |  |
| `last` | `Int` |  |

#### `transactions`

| Argument | Type | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `before` | `String` |  |
| `after` | `String` |  |
| `first` | `Int` |  |
| `last` | `Int` |  |

## Type `SteppedConsumptionRate`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

Unit rate which is valid within a consumption range.

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `gridOperatorCode` | `String` | Grid operator. |
| `regionOfOperation` | `String` | Region of operation. |
| `band` | `String` | The product rate band information. |
| `validFrom` | `DateTime` | Rate validity start. |
| `validTo` | `DateTime` | Rate validity end. |
| `unitType` | `UnitType` | What are we charging for (kWhs consumed, Days on supply, etc). |
| `pricePerUnit` | `Decimal!` | Price per kWh without tax. |
| `durationMonths` | `Int` | Duration in months for discount product rates. |
| `pricePerUnitIncTax` | `Decimal!` | Price per kWh including tax. |
| `timeOfUse` | `String` | Time of use label for the rate. |
| `stepStart` | `Int` | The kWh that the unit rate is valid from. |
| `stepEnd` | `Int` | The kWh that the unit rate is valid until. |

## Type `StopAutomatedPayments`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

Choose how automatic payments will be collected.

The possible errors that can be raised are:

- KT-CT-1111: Unauthorized.
- KT-CT-3822: Unauthorized.
- KT-CT-3968: Preference cannot be set this soon.
- KT-CT-3969: Preferences must change on a specific day of the week for weekly schedules.
- KT-CT-1113: Disabled GraphQL field requested.
- KT-CT-1112: 'Authorization' header not provided.

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `possibleErrors` | `[PossibleErrorType]` | Field with the possible errors of the query/mutation. Deprecated: The 'possibleErrors' field is deprecated. Please use the 'possibleErrors' query or the 'X-Kraken-Possible-Errors' header instead. - Marked as deprecated on 2025-01-31. - Scheduled for removal on or after 2025-03-01. |

## Type `SupplementaryLedgerType`

|---|---:|---|

| `name` | `String` | The display name of the ledger. |
| `ledgerType` | `String` |  |
| `number` | `String` | The canonical name of the ledger. |
| `currentBalance` | `Int` | The current final balance of the ledger in pence. |
| `paymentAdequacy` | `PaymentAdequacyDetailsType` |  |

## Type `SupplyPointType`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

Represents a SupplyPoint.

**Implements:** `CommonSupplyPointInterface`, `HasReadings`

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `id` | `ID!` | The ID of the supply point. |
| `marketName` | `String!` | The market this supply point belongs to. |
| `externalIdentifier` | `String` | The external identifier of the supply point. |
| `readings(startAt: DateTime!, endAt: DateTime!, readingType: ReadingTypes!, timeGranularity: TimeGranularities, timezone: String, units: [Units])` | `Readings` | Get readings from a readable device e.g., a supply point, device, or register. |
| `property` | `Property` | The supply point's property. |
| `devices(deviceIdentifiers: [String], before: String, after: String, first: Int, last: Int)` | `DevicesConnection` | Get list of devices under a supply point. |

### Field arguments

#### `readings`

| Argument | Type | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `startAt` | `DateTime!` | The inclusive start of the time period as an isoformatted string. |
| `endAt` | `DateTime!` | The exclusive end of the time period as an isoformatted string. |
| `readingType` | `ReadingTypes!` | Get readings of only this type. |
| `timeGranularity` | `TimeGranularities` | Time buckets to group readings into. |
| `timezone` | `String` | The timezone in which to return readings. Default: `"Asia/Tokyo"`. |
| `units` | `[Units]` | Optional filter to return readings only for specific list of units or unit (e.g., KILOWATT_HOURS, METERS_CUBED, LITER, etc.). |

#### `devices`

| Argument | Type | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `deviceIdentifiers` | `[String]` | The ids of the devices to get. If not provided, all devices for the supply point will be returned. |
| `before` | `String` |  |
| `after` | `String` |  |
| `first` | `Int` |  |
| `last` | `Int` |  |

## Type `SuspendDeviceControl`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

Suspend control of a device while away from home, e.g. on holiday.

This is to prevent charging during that period.

The possible errors that can be raised are:

- KT-CT-4301: Unable to find device for given account.
- KT-CT-4358: Unable to suspend device control.
- KT-CT-1113: Disabled GraphQL field requested.

| `krakenflexDevice` | `KrakenFlexDeviceType` |  |

| `expiryDate` | `Date` |  |

## Type `TeslaModifyScopes`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

A step which indicates that the Tesla permission scopes need to be granted by the user in order
to register their vehicle.

**Implements:** `SmartFlexOnboardingStepInterface`

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `id` | `ID` | A unique identifier for this onboarding step. |
| `modifyScopesUri` | `String` | Returns the URI to update the permission scopes. |
| `redirectUri` | `String` | The redirect URI to return to after updating the permission scopes. |

## Type `TeslaRegistrationFailed`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

A step which indicates that the Tesla registration failed and the user must
re-grant permission scopes to register their vehicle.

**Implements:** `SmartFlexOnboardingStepInterface`

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `id` | `ID` | A unique identifier for this onboarding step. |
| `modifyScopesSelected` | `Boolean` | Returns:- `true` if user wants to update permissions scopes.- `false` if user wants to retry the OAuth journey.- `null` if user has not made a decision. |

## Type `TeslaSetupVirtualKey`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

A step which indicates that the user must add the given URI as a virtual key in their Tesla account.

**Implements:** `SmartFlexOnboardingStepInterface`

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `id` | `ID` | A unique identifier for this onboarding step. |
| `key` | `String` | The name of the key to add. |
| `uri` | `String` | The URI to add as a virtual key in the user's Tesla account. |

## Type `TestCharge`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

A step which indicates if we're able to test charge the user's vehicle(s).

**Implements:** `SmartFlexOnboardingStepInterface`

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `id` | `ID` | A unique identifier for this onboarding step. |
| `error` | `TestChargeError` | Returns the error raised from a failed test charge. |
| `isReady` | `Boolean` | Returns `true` if the device is ready for a test charge. |
| `status` | `TestDispatchStatus` | Returns the dispatch status of test charge. Deprecated: The 'status' field is deprecated. Please use 'isStarted' and 'error' instead. - Marked as deprecated on 2024-12-12. - Scheduled for removal on or after 2025-02-12. |
| `isStarted` | `Boolean` | Returns `true` if the test charge has started. |

## Type `TestChargeError`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

Errors can be of two kinds (assuming the user is querying this type with `message`,
`reasons`, `errorType`, and `descriptions` fields):

1. If we were unable to start a test charge, the response will be:
     ```
     {
          "message": "TestChargeRefused",
          "reasons": [Returns a list of values from `TestChargeRefusalReason`],
          "errorType": "UNABLE_TO_INITIATE_TEST_CHARGE",
          "descriptions": [Returns a list of descriptions for the refusal reasons]
    }
    ```

    Where `descriptions` can be:
        - `DEVICE_LIVE`: "device is already live"
        - `DEVICE_ONBOARDING_IN_PROGRESS`: "test dispatch already in progress"
        - `DEVICE_RETIRED`: "device is retired"
        - `DEVICE_SUSPENDED`: "device is suspended"
        - `DEVICE_DISCONNECTED`: "device is disconnected"
        - `DEVICE_ALREADY_CHARGING`: "device is already charging"
        - `DEVICE_AWAY_FROM_HOME`: "device is away from home"
        - `DEVICE_NO_LOCATION_CONFIGURED`: "device has no location configured"
        - `DEVICE_LOCATION_UNABLE_TO_IDENTIFY`: "unable to identify device location"
        - `DEVICE_LOCATION_MISSING`: "device location is missing"

2. If an error occurred during a test charge, the response will be:
    ```
    {
        "message": "Test dispatch failed",
        "reasons": [Returns a list of values from `TestDispatchAssessmentFailureReason`],
        "errorType": "UNABLE_TO_COMPLETE_TEST_CHARGE",
        "descriptions": [Returns a list of descriptions for the refusal reasons]
    }
    ```

    Where `descriptions` can be:
        - `ASSESSMENTS_FAILED`: "Both power-based and charge status-based assessments failed"
        - `NOT_AT_HOME`: "Device location not at home"
        - `UNKNOWN`: "Unknown failure"
        - `UNABLE_TO_COMMUNICATE`: "Unable to communicate with device"
        - `DEVICE_DISCONNECTED`: "Device disconnected"
        - `SOC_LIMIT_REACHED`: "State of charge limit reached"
        - `ERROR`: "Could not fetch the test charge failure reason"
        - `NONE`: "No failure reason"

**Implements:** `SmartFlexOnboardingErrorInterface`

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `message` | `String` | A human readable error message. |
| `refusalReasons` | `[TestChargeRefusalReason]` | A list of refusal reasons from the failed test charge attempt. Deprecated: The 'refusalReasons' field is deprecated. Please use `errorType` and `reasons` instead. - Marked as deprecated on 2025-01-07. - Scheduled for removal on or after 2025-01-29. |
| `errorType` | `TestChargeErrorType` | The type of test charge error. |
| `reasons` | `[String]` | A list of reasons for a failed test charge attempt. |
| `descriptions` | `[String]` | A list of error descriptions for a failed test charge attempt. |

## Type `TransferLoyaltyPointsBetweenUsers`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

Transfer Loyalty Points between users.

The possible errors that can be raised are:

- KT-CT-1111: Unauthorized.
- KT-CT-9205: Insufficient Loyalty Points.
- KT-CT-9204: Negative or zero points set.
- KT-CT-9208: Invalid posted at datetime.
- KT-CT-9209: Negative Loyalty Points balance.
- KT-CT-9210: Unhandled Loyalty Points exception.
- KT-CT-1113: Disabled GraphQL field requested.
- KT-CT-1112: 'Authorization' header not provided.

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|

## Type `Trigger`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `id` | `String!` | The ID of the trigger. |
| `triggerTypeCode` | `String!` | The trigger type code for this trigger. |
| `createdAt` | `DateTime!` | The date/time that the trigger was created. |
| `accountsLinkedAt` | `DateTime` | The datetime that account linking was completed for this trigger. If null, the accounts field will be empty because linking hasn't occurred yet. |
| `accounts(before: String, after: String, first: Int, last: Int)` | `AccountConnectionTypeConnection!` | The accounts related to this trigger. |
| `messages(before: String, after: String, first: Int, last: Int)` | `MessageConnection!` | The messages created in response to this trigger. |
| `processingStatus` | `TriggerProcessingStatus!` | The current processing status of this trigger. |

### Field arguments

#### `accounts`

| Argument | Type | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `before` | `String` |  |
| `after` | `String` |  |
| `first` | `Int` |  |
| `last` | `Int` |  |

#### `messages`

| Argument | Type | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `before` | `String` |  |
| `after` | `String` |  |
| `first` | `Int` |  |
| `last` | `Int` |  |

## Type `UpdateAccountReferralStatus`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

Update the status of an AccountReferral.

Status transitions can only move forward:
- Pending -> Paid (allowed)
- Pending -> Cancelled (allowed)
- Paid -> any (not allowed)
- Cancelled -> any (not allowed)

The possible errors that can be raised are:

- KT-CT-6712: Invalid reference.
- KT-CT-6732: Invalid referral status transition.
- KT-CT-1113: Disabled GraphQL field requested.
- KT-CT-1111: Unauthorized.
- KT-CT-1112: 'Authorization' header not provided.

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `possibleErrors` | `[PossibleErrorType]` | Field with the possible errors of the query/mutation. Deprecated: The 'possibleErrors' field is deprecated. Please use the 'possibleErrors' query or the 'X-Kraken-Possible-Errors' header instead. - Marked as deprecated on 2025-01-31. - Scheduled for removal on or after 2025-03-01. |
| `accountReferral` | `ReferralType` | The updated account referral instance. |

## Type `UpdatePassword`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

Update user's password.

Note this is different from the ResetPassword mutation, which is used to reset a password
when the user has forgotten it. This mutation is used to update the password when the user
is already authenticated and wants to change their password.

Ideally, this mutation would simply receive a "new_password" and use the current password
update usecase, but, until we're ready for a breaking change, we'll need to use the
Django form to also validate the old password and two new passwords.

The possible errors that can be raised are:

- KT-CT-5460: Old password is invalid.
- KT-CT-5450: Password is invalid.
- KT-CT-1113: Disabled GraphQL field requested.

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `possibleErrors` | `[PossibleErrorType]` | Field with the possible errors of the query/mutation. Deprecated: The 'possibleErrors' field is deprecated. Please use the 'possibleErrors' query or the 'X-Kraken-Possible-Errors' header instead. - Marked as deprecated on 2025-01-31. - Scheduled for removal on or after 2025-03-01. |
| `viewer` | `AccountUser` | The currently authenticated user. This field requires the `Authorization` header to be set. |

## Type `UploadPostRequest`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

Information that should be used in the POST request to the S3 API.

For more details please see [this](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/s3-presigned-urls.html#generating-a-presigned-url-to-upload-a-file).

|---|---:|---|

| `fields` | `JSONString!` | Required fields for presigned post. |
| `key` | `String!` | S3 key for resigned post. |

## Type `UpsideDispatchMetaType`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `source` | `String` | Present for planned dispatches, otherwise `null`. Value can be `smart-charge`, `test-charge` or `bump-charge`. |
| `location` | `String` | Present for completed dispatches, otherwise `null`. The only relevant value is `AT_HOME` if present. |

## Type `UpsideDispatchType`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `start` | `DateTime!` | The start time of the dispatch. |
| `end` | `DateTime!` | The end time of the dispatch. |
| `startDt` | `String` | This field has been replaced by `start`. Deprecated: The 'startDt' field is deprecated. `start` has replaced `start_dt` for improved typing. - Marked as deprecated on 2024-04-19. - Scheduled for removal on or after 2025-01-01. |
| `endDt` | `String` | This field has been replaced by `end`. Deprecated: The 'endDt' field is deprecated. `end` has replaced `end_dt` for improved typing. - Marked as deprecated on 2024-04-19. - Scheduled for removal on or after 2025-01-01. |
| `deltaKwh` | `Int` | This field has been replaced by `delta`. Deprecated: The 'deltaKwh' field is deprecated. `delta` has replaced `deltaKwh` for increased precision. - Marked as deprecated on 2024-04-19. - Scheduled for removal on or after 2025-01-01. |
| `delta` | `Decimal` | Energy in kWh (import has a negative value). |
| `meta` | `UpsideDispatchMetaType` |  |

A type that returns a user action that needs to be completed for the device to be successfully registered.

| Field | Returns | Description |

## Type `UserActionRequired`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

A type that returns user actions that need to be completed for the device to be successfully registered

**Implements:** `SmartFlexOnboardingStepInterface`

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `id` | `ID` | A unique identifier for this onboarding step. |
| `userActions` | `[UserActionReason]` | List of actions the user must resolve in order for the device to be registered. |

## Type `UserInputRequired`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

A type that returns user inputs that need to be provided for the device to be successfully registered

**Implements:** `SmartFlexOnboardingStepInterface`

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `id` | `ID` | A unique identifier for this onboarding step. |
| `userInputs` | `[UserInputType]` | A list of requested inputs for a user to provide in order for the onboarding journey to continue. |
| `error` | `UserInputRequiredError` | An error describing which user inputs failed validation, if any. |

## Type `UserInputRequiredError`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

An error that occurred during validation of user inputs.

**Implements:** `SmartFlexOnboardingErrorInterface`

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `message` | `String` | A human readable error message. |

## Type `UserInputType`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `key` | `String` | Identifier for the requested user input. |
| `description` | `String` | Human friendly description of the requested input. |
| `value` | `String` | The value that has been submitted for this input. Can be a string, integer, or boolean. |
| `isRequired` | `Boolean` | True if the requested user input is required. |
| `isValidated` | `Boolean` | True if the submitted user input has been successfully validated. |
| `regex` | `String` | An optional regex pattern used to validate the user input. Uses Python re module syntax (evaluated with re.fullmatch). None if no pattern validation is applied. |
| `isVerified` | `Boolean` | Verification against the device happens after validation is successful. False means the input must be corrected. |

## Type `UserManagedPayment`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

Represents the preference of the user to pay himself, instead of get charged.

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `forPaymentsAfter` | `String` | The start time of the payment preference. |

## Type `ValidateAddress`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

Validate an address without persisting it. Accepts address form values (matching the structure from the addressFields query), runs validation, and returns the full descriptor with cleaned/normalised values and per-field errors so the client can compare submitted vs. normalised data.

The possible errors that can be raised are:

- KT-CT-4413: The supplied address field descriptor identifier is not recognised.
- KT-CT-1113: Disabled GraphQL field requested.

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `possibleErrors` | `[PossibleErrorType]` | Field with the possible errors of the query/mutation. Deprecated: The 'possibleErrors' field is deprecated. Please use the 'possibleErrors' query or the 'X-Kraken-Possible-Errors' header instead. - Marked as deprecated on 2025-01-31. - Scheduled for removal on or after 2025-03-01. |
| `isValid` | `Boolean!` | Whether the address passed all validation checks. |
| `cleanedDescriptor` | `AddressFieldDescriptorType` | The address field descriptor with cleaned/normalised values and per-field validation errors populated. Each field's errors list is empty when valid. Returned on both success and failure. |

## Type `ValidateEmail`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

Validate whether a user's email is a valid email via the Kickbox API.

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `isValid` | `Boolean` | Whether the email is valid or not. |

## Type `ValidateGPIN`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `isValid` | `Boolean!` | Whether the GPIN is valid. |

## Type `ValidateMfaDevice`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

Validate multi-factor authentication (MFA) devices for user.

The possible errors that can be raised are:

- KT-CT-1150: MFA device not found.
- KT-CT-1151: MFA device not found.
- KT-CT-1152: Invalid MFA token.
- KT-CT-1155: Enabled backup device is needed.
- KT-CT-1113: Disabled GraphQL field requested.
- KT-CT-1111: Unauthorized.
- KT-CT-1112: 'Authorization' header not provided.

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `possibleErrors` | `[PossibleErrorType]` | Field with the possible errors of the query/mutation. Deprecated: The 'possibleErrors' field is deprecated. Please use the 'possibleErrors' query or the 'X-Kraken-Possible-Errors' header instead. - Marked as deprecated on 2025-01-31. - Scheduled for removal on or after 2025-03-01. |
| `deviceIsValid` | `Boolean` | Flag to indicate if the device has been verified, so it can be used for MFA. |

## Type `ValidatePhone`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

Validate whether a user's phone number is a valid phone number.

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `isValid` | `Boolean` | Whether the phone number is valid or not. |

Paginator of Voice Campaigns

| `edges` | `[VoiceCampaignConnectionTypeEdge]!` | Contains the nodes in this connection. |
| `totalCount` | `Int!` | Total number of nodes. |
| `edgeCount` | `Int!` | Number of nodes in the edge. |

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

A Relay edge containing a `VoiceCampaignConnectionType` and its cursor.

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

Paginator of Campaign Items

| Field | Returns | Description |

| `edges` | `[VoiceCampaignItemConnectionTypeEdge]!` | Contains the nodes in this connection. |

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

A Relay edge containing a `VoiceCampaignItemConnectionType` and its cursor.

## Type `VoiceCampaignItemType`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `id` | `ID` | The ID of the campaign item. |
| `phoneNumber` | `String` | The phone number of the campaign item. This is used to determine the phone number to call when the item is processed. If it is not set, the account ID will be used instead to call the phone number associated with the account. |
| `status` | `CampaignItemStatus!` | The status of the campaign item that determines what actions can be taken on it. "UNASSIGNED": An item that is ready to be assigned to an agent "ASSIGNED": An item that is assigned to an agent and ready to contact "IN_PROGRESS": An item where a call is currently in progress "COMPLETE": An item has been contacted, and does not need to be re-contacted "REMOVED": An item that was removed without being contacted |
| `callWindowStart` | `DateTime` | Together with `call_window_end`, this determines the time window in which the item should be called. Specified as ISO 8601 format. |
| `callWindowEnd` | `DateTime` | Together with `call_window_start`, this determines the time window in which the item should be called. Specified as ISO 8601 format. |
| `accountId` | `ID` | The account ID associated with the item. If the phone number for the item is not set, the account ID will be used to determine the phone number to call. |
| `metadata` | `JSONString` | Metadata about the item. This enables items to be filtered based on additional information such as location. It is a dictionary of key-value pairs, with both keys and values being strings. |

## Type `VoiceCampaignType`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `id` | `ID` | The ID of the campaign. |
| `name` | `String!` | The name of the campaign. This is used to identify the campaign in the system, and must be unique. |
| `description` | `String` | Additional information to describe the purpose of the campaign. |
| `customOutboundPhoneNumber` | `String` | If appropriate, a custom number to use as the caller id for calls from this campaign. |
| `tags(before: String, after: String, first: Int, last: Int)` | `CallTagConnectionTypeConnection` | The call tags that can be used within this campaign. |
| `status` | `CampaignStatus` | The status of the campaign. Indicates whether calls can be made for items in the campaign or not. |
| `campaignType` | `TypeOfVoiceCampaign` | The type of campaign, e.g. preview or predictive. |
| `campaignItems(before: String, after: String, first: Int, last: Int)` | `VoiceCampaignItemConnectionTypeConnection` | The items that are part of the campaign. These are the items that contain information about what phone numbers or accounts to call while this campaign is active. |
| `preventDuplicateRecords` | `Boolean` | Flag to indicate whether not to create a duplicate record when uploading campaign items that already exist for a given account number and/or phone number |

### Field arguments

#### `tags`

| Argument | Type | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `before` | `String` |  |
| `after` | `String` |  |
| `first` | `Int` |  |
| `last` | `Int` |  |

#### `campaignItems`

| Argument | Type | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `before` | `String` |  |
| `after` | `String` |  |
| `first` | `Int` |  |
| `last` | `Int` |  |

## Type `VoucherPurchaseType`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `id` | `ID` | The purchase ID. |
| `charge` | `AccountChargeType` | The amount charged for the voucher. |
| `chargeBalanceTransfer` | `AccountBalanceTransferType` | A balance transfer for the voucher charge, if any. |
| `payment` | `AccountPaymentType` | The payment associated with the voucher purchase, if any. |
| `displayName` | `String` | Display name for the voucher purchase. |
| `clientParams` | `JSONString` | Additional metadata from client sources stored against the voucher. This data is not structural and won't be relied on by Kraken internally. |
| `purchasedAt` | `DateTime` | When the purchase was performed. |
| `voucherValue` | `Int` | The value of the voucher in cents. |
| `availableFrom` | `Date` | When the voucher becomes available to be redeemed. |
| `balance` | `Int` | The current balance left in the voucher. |
| `redemptions` | `[VoucherRedemptionType]` | Fetch the redemptions for this voucher. |
| `refundedAt` | `DateTime` | When the voucher was refunded, if it was refunded. |

## Type `VoucherRedemptionType`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `id` | `ID` | The redemption ID. |
| `credit` | `AccountCreditType` | The amount credited for the voucher redemption. |
| `claimedAt` | `DateTime` | When the redemption was claimed. |
| `isReversed` | `Boolean` | Whether the redemption has been reversed. |

## Type `VouchersBalanceDetail`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `redeemableToday` | `Int` | The balance from vouchers that can be redeemable today. |
| `redeemableInFuture` | `Int` | The balance from vouchers that will be redeemable in future. |

## Type `WaitForLiveIntegration`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

Indicates a step where the integration is pending activation.

Progress to the next onboarding step is blocked until the integration becomes active.

**Implements:** `SmartFlexOnboardingStepInterface`

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `id` | `ID` | A unique identifier for this onboarding step. |
| `isLive` | `Boolean` | Returns `true` if the device integration is supported by us, `false` otherwise. |

## Type `WaitForTransition`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

A generic step that can be used to hold an onboarding wizard until the device has
transitioned from one onboarding state to another.

Generally, this step should be completed via a KF AMI event handler after the
device has been registered and transitioned to the desired state.

**Implements:** `SmartFlexOnboardingStepInterface`

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `id` | `ID` | A unique identifier for this onboarding step. |

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

Withdraws an account.

The possible errors that can be raised are:

- KT-CT-1113: Disabled GraphQL field requested.

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `possibleErrors` | `[PossibleErrorType]` | Field with the possible errors of the query/mutation. Deprecated: The 'possibleErrors' field is deprecated. Please use the 'possibleErrors' query or the 'X-Kraken-Possible-Errors' header instead. - Marked as deprecated on 2025-01-31. - Scheduled for removal on or after 2025-03-01. |
| `accountNumber` | `String!` | The number of the account to withdraw. |

## Type `WithdrawDunning`

**Kind:** `OBJECT`

The possible errors that can be raised are:

- KT-CT-4178: No account found with given account number.
- KT-CT-11301: Account not in a dunning process for the given path name.
- KT-CT-11302: No active dunning process found.
- KT-CT-11303: Multiple active dunning processes found.
- KT-CT-11306: Withdrawing the dunning process failed.
- KT-CT-1113: Disabled GraphQL field requested.
- KT-CT-1111: Unauthorized.
- KT-CT-1112: 'Authorization' header not provided.

| Field | Returns | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `possibleErrors` | `[PossibleErrorType]` | Field with the possible errors of the query/mutation. Deprecated: The 'possibleErrors' field is deprecated. Please use the 'possibleErrors' query or the 'X-Kraken-Possible-Errors' header instead. - Marked as deprecated on 2025-01-31. - Scheduled for removal on or after 2025-03-01. |
| `withdrawSuccessful` | `Boolean` | Whether the dunning process was withdrawn successfully. |

## Type `BigInt`

**Kind:** `SCALAR`

The `BigInt` scalar type represents non-fractional whole numeric values.
`BigInt` is not constrained to 32-bit like the `Int` type and thus is a less
compatible type.

## Type `Boolean`

**Kind:** `SCALAR`

The `Boolean` scalar type represents `true` or `false`.

## Type `BusinessRoleString`

**Kind:** `SCALAR`

Graphene String that validates against the available business roles.

Available business roles:
- OWNER
- SUPPLIER

## Type `BusinessSectorString`

**Kind:** `SCALAR`

Graphene String that validates against the available business sectors.

Available business sectors:
No business sectors configured.

## Type `Date`

**Kind:** `SCALAR`

The `Date` scalar type represents a Date
value as specified by
[iso8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601).

## Type `DateTime`

**Kind:** `SCALAR`

The `DateTime` scalar type represents a DateTime
value as specified by
[iso8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601).

## Type `Decimal`

**Kind:** `SCALAR`

The `Decimal` scalar type represents a python Decimal.

## Type `Email`

**Kind:** `SCALAR`

## Type `Float`

**Kind:** `SCALAR`

The `Float` scalar type represents signed double-precision fractional values as specified by [IEEE 754](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IEEE_floating_point).

## Type `GenericScalar`

**Kind:** `SCALAR`

The `GenericScalar` scalar type represents a generic
GraphQL scalar value that could be:
String, Boolean, Int, Float, List or Object.

## Type `ID`

**Kind:** `SCALAR`

The `ID` scalar type represents a unique identifier, often used to refetch an object or as key for a cache. The ID type appears in a JSON response as a String; however, it is not intended to be human-readable. When expected as an input type, any string (such as `"4"`) or integer (such as `4`) input value will be accepted as an ID.

## Type `IndustryMidnightAlignedDateField`

**Kind:** `SCALAR`

Custom scalar that accepts a date string and returns a midnight-aligned,
industry timezone-aware datetime.

## Type `Int`

**Kind:** `SCALAR`

The `Int` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values. Int can represent values between -(2^31) and 2^31 - 1.

## Type `JSONString`

**Kind:** `SCALAR`

Allows use of a JSON String for input / output from the GraphQL schema.

Use of this type is *not recommended* as you lose the benefits of having a defined, static
schema (one of the key benefits of GraphQL).

## Type `NonEmptyID`

**Kind:** `SCALAR`

Custom scalar type representing a non-empty ID.

## Type `NonEmptyString`

**Kind:** `SCALAR`

Custom scalar type representing a non-empty string.

## Type `NormalizedDecimal`

**Kind:** `SCALAR`

A subclass of the Decimal type that normalizes the value before converting it into a string.

For example, "4.000" will be converted to "4" and "4.100" will be converted to "4.1".

## Type `RoleString`

**Kind:** `SCALAR`

Graphene String that validates against the available roles.

Available roles:
- ADMIN

## Type `String`

**Kind:** `SCALAR`

The `String` scalar type represents textual data, represented as UTF-8 character sequences. The String type is most often used by GraphQL to represent free-form human-readable text.

## Type `Time`

**Kind:** `SCALAR`

The `Time` scalar type represents a Time value as
specified by
[iso8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601).

## Type `URI`

**Kind:** `SCALAR`

## Type `UUID`

**Kind:** `SCALAR`

Leverages the internal Python implementation of UUID (uuid.UUID) to provide native UUID objects
in fields, resolvers and input.

## Type `AccountEvent`

**Kind:** `UNION`

**Possible types:** `EmailEventType`, `PrintEventType`, `SMSEventType`, `PushNotificationEventType`

## Type `ActionType`

**Kind:** `UNION`

Actions are things to do upon a user interaction, such as tapping a button.

**Possible types:** `DeeplinkActionType`, `LinkActionType`, `BackendScreenEventActionType`, `CloseActionType`, `ScreenActionType`, `ShowInputFieldErrorsActionType`

## Type `BackendScreenType`

**Kind:** `UNION`

A backend screen is the top-level container for mobile UI.

**Possible types:** `ComponentListType`, `GenericBackendScreen`, `Dashboard`, `FormScreenType`

## Type `CardItemType`

**Kind:** `UNION`

Items are sections making up a card.

**Possible types:** `TextType`, `ImageType`, `AnimationType`, `RectangularButtonType`, `PillButtonType`

## Type `CharacteristicValueUnion`

**Kind:** `UNION`

**Possible types:** `StringCharacteristicValueType`, `IntegerCharacteristicValueType`

## Type `ChargeDetail`

**Kind:** `UNION`

Supporting information about a charge

**Possible types:** `SupplyOrServiceCharge`

## Type `ContractParty`

**Kind:** `UNION`

Union type representing the subject that entered into a contract, either an Account or Business.

**Possible types:** `Account`, `BusinessType`

## Type `InitiateMoveOutResponse`

**Kind:** `UNION`

Returned when a customer initiates a Move Out from their MyPage.

**Possible types:** `MoveOutInitiated`, `CommonError`, `MoveOutDateInvalid`

## Type `InkConversationEvent`

**Kind:** `UNION`

**Possible types:** `InkNewMessage`

## Type `InkMessage`

**Kind:** `UNION`

**Possible types:** `InkEmail`, `InkSMS`, `InkLine`, `InkWhatsApp`, `InkTwilioWhatsApp`, `InkPost`, `InkGenericMessage`, `InkLiveChatMessage`

## Type `IntegrationResponse`

**Kind:** `UNION`

**Possible types:** `Integration`, `LineIntegration`

## Type `ItemSizeType`

**Kind:** `UNION`

**Possible types:** `FractionSizeType`, `PointsSizeType`

## Type `ItemType`

**Kind:** `UNION`

Items are sections making up a screen. They can be different types, hence Union.

**Possible types:** `TextType`, `ImageType`, `AnimationType`, `RectangularButtonType`, `PillButtonType`, `CardComponentType`

## Type `LegacyOrderCustomerType`

**Kind:** `UNION`

Union type representing a customer, either an account or a business.

**Possible types:** `Account`, `BusinessType`

## Type `LegacyOrderLinePeriodType`

**Kind:** `UNION`

Union type representing either a date range or a duration for an order line period.

**Possible types:** `LegacyOrderLineDateRangeType`, `LegacyOrderLineDurationType`

## Type `LineMessage`

**Kind:** `UNION`

**Possible types:** `LineTextMessage`, `LineStickerMessage`, `LineImageMessage`

## Type `LinkUserToLineResponse`

**Kind:** `UNION`

**Possible types:** `LineLinkRedirectResponse`, `LinkTokenNotFound`, `AlreadyLinkedError`

## Type `PaymentPreferenceUnion`

**Kind:** `UNION`

One of PreferredInstructionType or UserManagedPaymentType

**Possible types:** `PreferredInstruction`, `UserManagedPayment`

## Type `Product`

**Kind:** `UNION`

**Possible types:** `ElectricitySingleStepProduct`, `ElectricitySteppedProduct`, `ElectricityFitProduct`, `GasTieredProduct`

## Type `QuoteCostUnionType`

**Kind:** `UNION`

**Possible types:** `DecimalType`, `FloatType`, `IntegerType`, `QuantityType`, `StringType`

## Type `QuotedSupplyPoint`

**Kind:** `UNION`

DEPRECATED - A single quoted supply point for a customers quote request.

**Possible types:** `QuotedElectricitySupplyPoint`, `QuotedElectricityGenerationPoint`

## Type `RegisterDemandResponseCampaignParticipantResponse`

**Kind:** `UNION`

Returned when a customer registers for a Demand Response campaign.

**Possible types:** `RegisterDemandResponseCampaignParticipantRegistered`, `CommonError`

## Type `SalesRecordType`

**Kind:** `UNION`

A union type representing different kinds of sales records.

**Possible types:** `KrakenDrivenSalesInfoType`, `KrakenTrackedSalesInfoType`, `ExternalSalesInfoType`

## Type `SectionContent`

**Kind:** `UNION`

**Possible types:** `CardComponentType`, `CarouselItemType`

## Type `SmartFlexChargingProblem`

**Kind:** `UNION`

**Possible types:** `SmartFlexChargingError`, `SmartFlexChargingTruncation`

## Type `SpecialCircumstanceRecordUnion`

**Kind:** `UNION`

**Possible types:** `SpecialCircumstanceRecordType`, `TemporarySpecialCircumstanceRecordType`

## Type `StoreRefundBankAccountResponse`

**Kind:** `UNION`

Returned when a customer stores a refund bank account from their MyPage.

**Possible types:** `RefundBankAccountStored`, `CommonError`

## Type `SuggestFixedPaymentAmountResponse`

**Kind:** `UNION`

Returned when a customer creates a fixed payment schedule from their MyPage.

**Possible types:** `FixedPaymentAmountSuggested`, `CommonError`

## Type `SwitchInCorrectionResponse`

**Kind:** `UNION`

Returned when a customer self-services a correction of switch-in data.

**Possible types:** `SwitchInCorrectionPerformed`

## Type `SwitchToProductWithFixedFuelCostAdjustmentResponse`

**Kind:** `UNION`

Returned when a customer switches to the fixed FCA product on the consumer site.

**Possible types:** `ProductWithFixedFuelCostAdjustmentSwitched`, `CommonError`

## Type `UnlinkUserFromLineResponse`

**Kind:** `UNION`

**Possible types:** `LineUnlinkedResponse`, `LinkTokenNotFound`, `LineCommonError`

## Type `UpdateDemandResponseCampaignParticipantTipResponse`

**Kind:** `UNION`

**Possible types:** `DemandResponseCampaignParticipantTipUpdated`, `CommonError`

## Type `UtilityFiltersOutput`

**Kind:** `UNION`

**Possible types:** `ElectricityFiltersOutput`, `EmbeddedElectricityFiltersOutput`, `GasFiltersOutput`, `WaterFiltersOutput`

# Directives

## Directive `@include`

Directs the executor to include this field or fragment only when the `if` argument is true.

**Locations:** `FIELD`, `FRAGMENT_SPREAD`, `INLINE_FRAGMENT`

| Argument | Type | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `if` | `Boolean!` | Included when true. |

## Directive `@skip`

Directs the executor to skip this field or fragment when the `if` argument is true.

**Locations:** `FIELD`, `FRAGMENT_SPREAD`, `INLINE_FRAGMENT`

| Argument | Type | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `if` | `Boolean!` | Skipped when true. |

## Directive `@deprecated`

Marks an element of a GraphQL schema as no longer supported.

**Locations:** `FIELD_DEFINITION`, `ARGUMENT_DEFINITION`, `INPUT_FIELD_DEFINITION`, `ENUM_VALUE`

| Argument | Type | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `reason` | `String` | Explains why this element was deprecated, usually also including a suggestion for how to access supported similar data. Formatted using the Markdown syntax, as specified by [CommonMark](https://commonmark.org/). Default: `"No longer supported"`. |

## Directive `@specifiedBy`

Exposes a URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

**Locations:** `SCALAR`

| Argument | Type | Description |
|---|---:|---|
| `url` | `String!` | The URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar. |

## Directive `@oneOf`

Indicates an Input Object is a OneOf Input Object.

**Locations:** `INPUT_OBJECT`
